2026-06-23.patch_summary: This patch introduces a per-page_counter stock, which allows each page counter to have its own cache of pre-charged pages. This addresses issues with the current implementation, including random eviction of memcgs when there are more than 7 competing for CPU resources, and tight coupling between stock and memcg. The new design enables separate stock caches for other page counters and simplifies charging logic. Stock usage and refills now happen via lockless atomic operations, eliminating the need for asynchronous workqueues.
